response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with ~15 years of experience, but the profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. Modern C++ experience is minimal — a brief 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project — while the bulk of the career is web/full-stack work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no evidence of IEC 62443/SDL experience, MQTT, industrial protocols, message-broker architectures at scale in C++, or industrial automation domain exposure, all of which are core to this position.
strengths: Deep overall engineering experience with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby (millions of requests/sec, async I/O, fibers)
Proven mentorship and knowledge-sharing track record (workshops, dojos, courses)
concerns: Almost no recent or sustained professional C++/STL experience (last hands-on C++ role ended in 2015)
No industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443), SDL, MQTT, or automation-protocol experience
Career trajectory is web/full-stack and product-focused, not embedded/industrial systems architecture

key_factors.factor: Strong general seniority and systems-performance experience
